req.title: Open checking account (negative variant)
req.statement: A verified customer shall be able to open a checking account with an initial deposit between $25 and $50,000; deposits outside this range shall be rejected. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines a deposit range of [$25, $50,000] for checking account creation. I identified four equivalence partitions: valid range (EP-positive), below minimum (EP-negative), above maximum (EP-negative), and unverified customer (NEG/DT). Boundary values at exactly $25 (lower bound) and $50,000 (upper bound) are critical. I also cover the state where a customer transitions from 'no account' to 'account created' with a welcome notification dispatched (STC). The unverified customer path exercises the 403 authorization error. Together these seven tests satisfy the minimum coverage mandate and trace directly to R-FIN-051 clauses.

tests.name: test_open_checking_valid_deposit_mid_range
preconditions: deposit amount $1000 is within valid range [$25, $50000]
actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified customer token and initial_deposit=1000
trace: R-FIN-051: valid deposit range $25–$50000 shall create account
tests.executable:
def test_open_checking_valid_deposit_mid_range(verified_customer_client, assert_notification_dispatched):
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 1000, "account_type": "checking"}
    resp = verified_customer_client.post("/accounts/checking", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" in body
    assert body.get("account_type") == "checking"
    assert_notification_dispatched(body["account_id"])

tests.name: test_open_checking_deposit_below_minimum_rejected
preconditions: deposit amount $10 is below valid minimum of $25
actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified customer token and initial_deposit=10
expected: response body contains error code indicating deposit too low
trace: R-FIN-051: deposits outside range shall be rejected with 422
tests.executable:
def test_open_checking_deposit_below_minimum_rejected(verified_customer_client):
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 10, "account_type": "checking"}
    resp = verified_customer_client.post("/accounts/checking", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body
    error_text = str(body.get("error", body.get("detail", ""))).lower()
    assert any(kw in error_text for kw in ["deposit", "minimum", "range"])

tests.name: test_open_checking_deposit_above_maximum_rejected
preconditions: deposit amount $75000 exceeds valid maximum of $50000
actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified customer token and initial_deposit=75000
expected: response body contains error indicating deposit exceeds limit
trace: R-FIN-051: deposits outside range shall be rejected with 422
tests.executable:
def test_open_checking_deposit_above_maximum_rejected(verified_customer_client):
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 75000, "account_type": "checking"}
    resp = verified_customer_client.post("/accounts/checking", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body
    error_text = str(body.get("error", body.get("detail", ""))).lower()
    assert any(kw in error_text for kw in ["deposit", "maximum", "limit", "range"])

tests.name: test_open_checking_deposit_at_lower_boundary_accepted
preconditions: deposit amount $25 is exactly the lower boundary
actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified customer token and initial_deposit=25
trace: R-FIN-051: lower boundary $25 is inclusive – account shall be created
tests.executable:
def test_open_checking_deposit_at_lower_boundary_accepted(verified_customer_client, assert_notification_dispatched):
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 25, "account_type": "checking"}
    resp = verified_customer_client.post("/accounts/checking", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" in body
    assert_notification_dispatched(body["account_id"])

tests.name: test_open_checking_deposit_below_lower_boundary_rejected
preconditions: deposit amount $24 is one unit below the lower boundary of $25
actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified customer token and initial_deposit=24
trace: R-FIN-051: BVA lower bound off-point ($24) must be rejected
R-FIN-051: deposits outside range shall be rejected with 422

tests.name: test_open_checking_deposit_at_upper_boundary_accepted
preconditions: deposit amount $50000 is exactly the upper boundary
actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified customer token and initial_deposit=50000
trace: R-FIN-051: upper boundary $50000 is inclusive – account shall be created
tests.executable:
def test_open_checking_deposit_at_upper_boundary_accepted(verified_customer_client, assert_notification_dispatched):
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 50000, "account_type": "checking"}
    resp = verified_customer_client.post("/accounts/checking", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" in body
    assert_notification_dispatched(body["account_id"])

tests.name: test_open_checking_unverified_customer_forbidden
preconditions: deposit amount $500 would otherwise be valid
actions: POST /accounts/checking with unverified customer token and initial_deposit=500
trace: R-FIN-051: error pathway – 403 when customer not verified
R-FIN-051: precondition customer.verified=true must hold for success
tests.executable:
def test_open_checking_unverified_customer_forbidden(unverified_customer_client):
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 500, "account_type": "checking"}
    resp = unverified_customer_client.post("/accounts/checking", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body
    error_text = str(body.get("error", body.get("detail", ""))).lower()
    assert any(kw in error_text for kw in ["forbidden", "unauthorized", "verified", "not allowed"])

tests.name: test_open_checking_account_state_transition_no_account_to_active
preconditions: customer has no existing checking account (state: NO_ACCOUNT)
deposit amount $500 is within valid range
actions: Assert GET /accounts/checking returns 404 confirming NO_ACCOUNT state
Assert account transitions to ACTIVE state via GET /accounts/checking
trace: R-FIN-051: account created trigger causes state transition NO_ACCOUNT -> ACTIVE
R-FIN-051: STC covers the state change triggered by account opening
tests.executable:
def test_open_checking_account_state_transition_no_account_to_active(verified_customer_client, unique_customer_token):
    client = verified_customer_client
    pre = client.get("/accounts/checking")
    assert pre.status_code == 404, "Precondition: customer must have no checking account"
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 500, "account_type": "checking"}
    create_resp = client.post("/accounts/checking", json=payload)
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201
    account_id = create_resp.json()["account_id"]
    post = client.get(f"/accounts/checking/{account_id}")
    assert post.status_code == 200
    assert post.json().get("status") == "active"

tests.name: test_open_checking_decision_table_verified_valid_deposit
preconditions: decision table row: verified=Y, valid_deposit=Y -> outcome=CREATED
actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified token and initial_deposit=1500
trace: R-FIN-051: DT row (verified=Y, deposit_in_range=Y) -> account created + notification
R-FIN-051: decision table covers all condition combinations
